aquacroptools 0.2.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Getting Started: Initializing an AquaCrop Project
    • Onset of the Growing Season: Methods, Limitations, and Sahelian Realities
    • Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacroptools

Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File

Source: R/write_cal.R
write_cal.Rd

Creates an AquaCrop calendar file (.CAL) defining the onset of the growing period. Three onset types are supported: a fixed calendar day, a rainfall-based criterion, or a thermal criterion.

write_cal(
  site_name,
  onset,
  fixed_day = NULL,
  window_start = NULL,
  window_length = NULL,
  criterion = NULL,
  preset_value = NULL,
  successive_days = NULL,
  occurrences = 1L,
  cum_rain_upper = NULL,
  wet_days_lower = NULL,
  wet_days_upper = NULL,
  dry_spell_lower = NULL,
  dry_spell_upper = NULL,
  fuzzy_threshold = NULL,
  path = "CAL/",
  description = NULL,
  version = 7.1,
  eol = NULL
)

Arguments

site_name

Character. Name of the CAL file (without extension). Typically the station name.

onset

Character. Onset type: "fixed", "rainfall", or "thermal".

fixed_day

Integer. Calendar day (1-366) for the onset of the growing period. Required when onset is "fixed".

window_start

Integer. Day-number (1-366) of the start of the time window. Required when onset is "rainfall" or "thermal".

window_length

Integer. Length of the time window in days. Required when onset is "rainfall" or "thermal".

criterion

Integer. Criterion number within the onset group (1-4 for thermal; 1-5 for rainfall). Rainfall criteria (onset = "rainfall"):

1

Cumulative rainfall since start of window >= preset (mm).

2

Observed rainfall in N successive days >= preset (mm). Requires successive_days.

3

10-day rainfall >= preset (mm).

4

10-day rainfall >= preset fraction of 10-day ETo (percent).

5

Fuzzy logic index: cumulative rainfall x wet days x dry spell >= fuzzy_threshold. Requires preset_value (cum_rain_lower), successive_days (accum_days), and all cum_rain_upper, wet_days_lower, wet_days_upper, dry_spell_lower, dry_spell_upper, fuzzy_threshold.

Thermal criteria (onset = "thermal"):

1

Daily Tmin in each of N successive days >= preset (degC). Requires successive_days.

2

Daily Tmean in each of N successive days >= preset (degC). Requires successive_days.

3

Cumulative GDD in N successive days >= preset (degC). Requires successive_days.

4

Cumulative GDD since start of window >= preset (degC).

preset_value

Numeric. Threshold value for the criterion. Unit: mm for rainfall criteria 1-3, percent for criterion 4, degC for all thermal criteria. For criterion 5 (fuzzy): lower bound of cumulative rainfall (mm), i.e. cum_rain_lower.

successive_days

Integer. Number of consecutive days. Required for rainfall criteria 2 and 5 (where it represents accum_days) and thermal criteria 1, 2, and 3. Ignored for all other criteria. Default: NULL.

occurrences

Integer. Number of times the criterion must be met before onset is triggered (1-3). Ignored for criterion 5. Default: 1.

cum_rain_upper

Numeric. Upper bound of cumulative rainfall (mm) for the fuzzy gamma_1 function. Required for criterion 5. Must be > preset_value.

wet_days_lower

Integer. Lower bound of wet-day count for the fuzzy gamma_2 function. Required for criterion 5.

wet_days_upper

Integer. Upper bound of wet-day count for the fuzzy gamma_2 function. Required for criterion 5. Must be > wet_days_lower.

dry_spell_lower

Integer. Lower bound of longest dry spell (days) for the fuzzy gamma_3 function. Required for criterion 5.

dry_spell_upper

Integer. Upper bound of longest dry spell (days) for the fuzzy gamma_3 function. Required for criterion 5. Must be > dry_spell_lower.

fuzzy_threshold

Numeric between 0 and 1. Defuzzification threshold. Required for criterion 5.

path

Output directory path for CAL files. Default: "CAL/".

description

Character. Description on the first line. Default: auto-generated based on onset type and criterion.

version

Numeric. AquaCrop version number. Default: 7.1.

eol

End-of-line character style. One of "windows", "linux", "macos". If NULL (default), auto-detected from the system.

Value

Invisibly returns the output file path.

Details

User-facing criterion numbers run from 1 to 4 within each onset group (1 to 5 for rainfall). They are converted internally to AquaCrop's numbering scheme (rainfall passes through as-is; thermal criterion c becomes c + 10).

References

Waongo, M., Laux, P., Traoré, S.B., Sanon, M., & Kunstmann, H. (2014). A crop model and fuzzy rule based approach for optimizing maize planting dates in Burkina Faso, West Africa. Journal of Applied Meteorology and Climatology, 53(3), 598–613. doi:10.1175/JAMC-D-13-0116.1

See also

Other AquaCrop file writers: write_cli(), write_cro(), write_eto(), write_gwt(), write_irr(), write_man(), write_obs(), write_off(), write_plu(), write_prm(), write_swo(), write_tnx()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Fixed onset on day 212
write_cal(
  site_name = "station_01",
  onset     = "fixed",
  fixed_day = 212
)

# Rainfall criterion 1: cumulative rainfall >= 50 mm
write_cal(
  site_name     = "station_01",
  onset         = "rainfall",
  window_start  = 121,
  window_length = 92,
  criterion     = 1,
  preset_value  = 50,
  occurrences   = 1
)

# Rainfall criterion 2: observed rainfall >= 30 mm in 3 successive days
write_cal(
  site_name       = "station_01",
  onset           = "rainfall",
  window_start    = 121,
  window_length   = 92,
  criterion       = 2,
  preset_value    = 30,
  successive_days = 3,
  occurrences     = 1
)

# Rainfall criterion 3: 10-day rainfall >= 40 mm
write_cal(
  site_name     = "station_01",
  onset         = "rainfall",
  window_start  = 121,
  window_length = 92,
  criterion     = 3,
  preset_value  = 40,
  occurrences   = 1
)

# Rainfall criterion 4: 10-day rainfall >= 50 percent of ETo
write_cal(
  site_name     = "station_01",
  onset         = "rainfall",
  window_start  = 121,
  window_length = 92,
  criterion     = 4,
  preset_value  = 50,
  occurrences   = 1
)

# Rainfall criterion 5: fuzzy logic onset (Waongo et al. 2014)
write_cal(
  site_name       = "station_01",
  onset           = "rainfall",
  window_start    = 121,
  window_length   = 92,
  criterion       = 5,
  preset_value    = 20,   # cum_rain_lower
  successive_days = 3,    # accum_days
  cum_rain_upper  = 40,
  wet_days_lower  = 1,
  wet_days_upper  = 3,
  dry_spell_lower = 7,
  dry_spell_upper = 15,
  fuzzy_threshold = 0.5
)

# Thermal criterion 1: daily Tmin >= 5 degC for 4 successive days
write_cal(
  site_name       = "station_01",
  onset           = "thermal",
  window_start    = 1,
  window_length   = 60,
  criterion       = 1,
  preset_value    = 5.0,
  successive_days = 4,
  occurrences     = 1
)

# Thermal criterion 2: daily Tmean >= 10 degC for 3 successive days
write_cal(
  site_name       = "station_01",
  onset           = "thermal",
  window_start    = 1,
  window_length   = 60,
  criterion       = 2,
  preset_value    = 10.0,
  successive_days = 3,
  occurrences     = 1
)

# Thermal criterion 3: cumulative GDD >= 20 degC in 8 successive days
write_cal(
  site_name       = "station_01",
  onset           = "thermal",
  window_start    = 1,
  window_length   = 60,
  criterion       = 3,
  preset_value    = 20.0,
  successive_days = 8,
  occurrences     = 1
)

# Thermal criterion 4: cumulative GDD since start >= 600 degC
write_cal(
  site_name     = "station_01",
  onset         = "thermal",
  window_start  = 1,
  window_length = 60,
  criterion     = 4,
  preset_value  = 600.0,
  occurrences   = 1
)
} # }

Contents
